Name: Aggron
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Aggron by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: You can tell its age by the length of its iron horns. It claims an entire mountain as its territory.

Name: Altaria
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It flies gracefully through the sky. Its melodic humming makes you feel like you’re in a dream.

Name: Celebi
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of either player’s deck and put them back on top of that player’s deck in any order.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon wanders across time. Grass and trees flourish in the forests in which it has appeared.

Name: Drapion
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Flavor-Text: It attacks people and Pokémon that cross the desert. This has only furthered its bad reputation.

Name: Mamoswine
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A frozen Mamoswine was dug from ice dating back 10,000 years. This Pokémon has been around a long, long, long time.

Name: Nidoking
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Nidoking gets +20 HP for each Nidoqueen you have in play.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its tail is thick and powerful. If it binds an enemy, it can render the victim helpless quite easily.

Name: Porygon-Z
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. This power can’t be used if Porygon-Z is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If Porygon-Z has no Rainbow Energy attached to it, Porygon-Z does 20 damage to itself and Porygon-Z is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its programming was modified to enable it to travel through alien dimensions. Seems there might have been an error…

Name: Rapidash
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: At full gallop, its four hooves barely touch the ground because it moves so incredibly fast.

Name: Solrock
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have Lunatone in play, damage counters can’t be removed from any Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Damage counters can still be moved.)
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

Name: Spiritomb
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Spiritomb from your hand onto your Bench, you may use this power. Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 6 cards.
Effects: Choose [G][R][W][L][P][F][D][M] or [C] type. Put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon your opponent has in play of the type you chose.
Flavor-Text: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.

Name: Venomoth
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, your Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can’t be used if Venomoth is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The powder on its wings is poisonous if it is dark in hue. If it is light in hue, it causes paralysis.

Name: Victreebel
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Victreebel is your Active Pokémon, your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C][C] more.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned. Remove 3 damage counters from Victreebel.
Flavor-Text: Acid that has dissolved many prey becomes sweeter, making it even more effective at attracting prey.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Choose 2 cards from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at the cards you chose, then have your opponent shuffle those cards into his or her deck.
Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards from your hand, this attack does nothing.)
Flavor-Text: They live on large trees. They are said to communicate by connecting their tails to those of others.

Name: Banette
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon and put it in the Lost Zone.
Count the number of cards in your opponent’s hand. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: Search your deck for both halves of a Pokémon LEGEND, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Move an Energy card attached to Bronzong to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Remove 3 damage counters from Carnivine. The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It walks around on its tentacles in search of a tree branch where it can dangle down and ambush prey.

Name: Ditto
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: The number of Benched Pokémon your opponent can have is now 4. If your opponent has 5 Benched Pokémon, your opponent must discard 1 of them and all cards attached to it.
Flavor-Text: Its transformation ability is perfect. However, if made to laugh, it can’t maintain its disguise.

Name: Dragonite
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions from Dragonite.
Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing. If both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: This marine Pokémon has an impressive build that lets it freely fly over raging seas without trouble.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects: Flip a coin for each [F] Energy attached to Dugtrio. This attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Extremely powerful, they can dig through even the hardest ground to a depth of over 60 miles.

Name: Electivire
Effects: Search your discard pile for a [L] Energy card and attach it to Electivire.
This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Energy cards attached to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: As its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.

Name: Elekid
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Elekid is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Elekid by attacks.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance. Elekid is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Even in the most vicious storm, this Pokémon plays happily if thunder rumbles in the sky.

Name: Golduck
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever you attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to Golduck, remove 2 damage counters from Golduck.
Effects: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

Name: Grumpig
Effects: Your opponent can’t use any Poké-Powers on his or her Pokémon during his or her next turn.
Your opponent flips a coin for each of his or her Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage times the number of tails. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It can perform odd dance steps to influence foes. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

Name: Kricketune
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: By allowing its cry to resonate in the hollow of its belly, it produces a captivating sound.

Name: Lunatone
Effects: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Lunatone does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

Name: Machamp
Effects: You may do 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If you do, Machamp does 20 damage to itself.
Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each [F] Energy attached to Machamp.
Flavor-Text: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: For each [R] Energy attached to Magmortar, discard the top card from your opponent’s deck. Then, flip a coin. If tail, discard all [R] Energy attached to Magmortar.
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit out of its arms. Its breath also sears and sizzles.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
If you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It uses its scaly, rugged body to seal the entrance of its nest and protect its young from predators.

Name: Pidgeot
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the attack cost of each of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks is [C][C] more.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It spreads its beautiful wings wide to frighten its enemies. It can fly at Mach 2 speed.

Name: Sharpedo
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent discards all card from his or her hand.
Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Sharpedo.
Flavor-Text: It can swim at speeds of 75 mph by jetting seawater through its body. It is the bandit of the sea.

Name: Wailord
Effects: Flip 2 coins damage. For each heads, remove 3 damage counters from Wailord.
Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Wailord. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Wailord, this attack does 50 damage plus 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is the largest of all identified Pokémon. They jump as a pack to herd their prey.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its crystalline orbs appear to give this Pokémon the power to freely control the weather.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all [L] Energy attached to Electabuzz.
Flavor-Text: Its body constantly discharges electricity. Getting close to it will make your hair stand on end.

Name: Electrode
Effects: You may do 40 damage plus 60 more damage. If you do, discard all [L] Energy attached to Electrode.
Flavor-Text: It is dangerous. If it has too much electricity and has nothing to do, it amuses itself by exploding.

Name: Haunter
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its tongue is made of gas. If licked, its victim starts shaking constantly until death eventually comes.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: If it is safe, the young gets out of the belly pouch to play. The adult keeps a close eye on the youngster.

Name: Lairon
Effects: Lairon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It loves iron ore. Groups of them fight for territory by bashing one another with their steel bodies.

Name: Lickilicky
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon for each Energy attached to Lickilicky. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Remove 2 damage counters from Lickilicky. The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has space in its throat to store saliva. It can also roll up its tongue and store it in the same spot.

Name: Luvdisc
Effects: Each player draws and reveals the top card of his or her deck.
Before doing damage, remove 1 damage counter from the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its heart-shaped body makes it popular. In some places, you would give a Luvdisc to someone you love.

Name: Machoke
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It always goes at its full power, but this very tough and durable Pokémon never gets tired.

Name: Magby
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Magby is Asleep, prevents all damage done to Magby by attacks.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned. Magby is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is found in volcanic craters. Its body temperature is over 1,100 degrees Fahrenheit, so don’t underestimate it.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Each player discards the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Energy cards discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: The fiery surface of its body gives off a wavering, rippling glare that is similar to the sun.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The Magnemite are united by a magnetism so powerful, it dries all moisture in its vicinity.

Name: Marowak
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.
Flavor-Text: It has been seen pounding boulders with the bone it carries in order to tap out messages to others.

Name: Nidorina
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a calm and caring nature. Because its horn grows slowly, it prefers not to fight.

Name: Nidorino
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Quick to anger, it stabs enemies with its horn to inject a powerful poison when it becomes agitated.

Name: Pidgeotto
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It renders its prey immobile using well-developed claws, then carries the prey more than 60 miles to its nest.

Name: Piloswine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Because the long hair all over its body obscures its sight, it just keeps charging repeatedly.

Name: Porygon2
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Porygon2 from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Further research enhanced its abilities. Sometimes, it may exhibit motions that were not programmed.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects: If Tentacruel evolved from Tentacool during this turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In battle, it extends all 80 of its tentacles to entrap its opponent inside a poisonous net.

Name: Unown
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Unown from your hand onto your Bench, remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

Name: Wailmer
Effects: Choose 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It bounces playfully like a ball. The more seawater it swallows, the higher it bounces.

Name: Weepinbell
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Filp a coin. If heads, look at your opponent’s hand, choose 1 card, and discard it.
Flavor-Text: Even though it is filled with acid, it does not melt because it also oozes a protective fluid.

Name: Yanmega
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Switch Yanmega with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The beat of its wings is so powerful that it accidentally dislodges full-grown trees when it takes off in flight.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Draw cards until you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent.
Flavor-Text: Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.

Name: Aron
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck. Then, remove 2 damage counters from Aron.
Flavor-Text: When it evolves, it sheds the steel carapace that covered its whole body and develops a new one.

Name: Bellsprout
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Bellsprout does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Bronzor during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

Name: Carvanha
Effects: During your next turn, Carvanha’s Bite attack’s base damage is 40.
Flavor-Text: They form packs to attack boats and rip out their hulls to sink them. They live in rivers in the jungle.

Name: Cubone
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Any damage done to Cubone by your opponent’s attacks is reduced by 20 for each Marowak in your discard pile (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: If it is sad or lonely, the skull it wears shakes and emits a plaintive and mournful sound.

Name: Diglett
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Diglett during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: If a Diglett digs through a field, it leaves the soil perfectly tilled and ideal for planting crops.

Name: Dratini
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is full of life energy. It continually sheds its skin and grows steadily larger.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of gas. Despite lacking substance, it can envelop an opponent of any size and cause suffocation.

Name: Illumise
Effects: Remove 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin for each Volbeat you have in play. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its fragrance attracts a swarm of Volbeat, so they draw over 200 patterns in the night sky.

Name: Kricketot
Flavor-Text: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its long tongue, slathered with a gooey saliva, sticks to anything, so it is very useful.

Name: Machop
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Always brimming with power, it passes time by lifting boulders. Doing so makes it even stronger.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Switch Magnemite with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The units at the sides of its body generate antigravity energy to keep it aloft in the air.

Name: Nidoran Female
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose 1 Pokémon you find there, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Although not very combative, it will torment its foes with poison spikes if it is threatened in any way.

Name: Nidoran Male
Effects: If Nidoran Female is on your Bench, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It raises its big ears to check its surroundings. It will strike first if it senses any danger.

Name: Pidgey
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle Pidgey and all cards attached to it back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Common in grassy areas and forests, it is very docile and will chase off enemies by flapping up sand.

Name: Ponyta
Flavor-Text: It is a weak runner immediately after birth. It gradually becomes faster by chasing after its parents.

Name: Porygon
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to Porygon and remove 4 damage counters from Porygon.
Flavor-Text: A manmade Pokémon that came about as a result of research. It is programmed with only basic motions.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: Filp a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has mystical powers but doesn’t recall that it has used them. That is why it always looks puzzled.

Name: Shuppet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Put 1 damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its horn to feed on envy and malice, or so it’s said. It’s very active at night.

Name: Skorupi
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.

Name: Spoink
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Switch Spoink with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It bounces around on its tail to keep its heart pumping. It carries a pearl from Clamperl on its head.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its wings bring cottony clouds to mind. It grooms with springwater and loves to sit on heads.

Name: Swinub
Flavor-Text: It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

Name: Tentacool
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It drifts aimlessly in waves. Very difficult to see in water, it may not be noticed until it stings.

Name: Venonat
Effects: Remove from Venonat the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Poison oozes from all over its body. It catches and eats small bugs at night that are attracted by light.

Name: Volbeat
Effects: If you don’t have Illumise in play, this attack does nothing. Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Flavor-Text: It emits light from its tail to communicate. It loves the sweet aroma given off by Illumise.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, Voltorb does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

Name: Yanma
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Yanma has no Energy attached to it, Yanma’s Retreat Cost is 0.
Flavor-Text: Its large eyes can scan 360 degrees. It looks in all directions to seek out insects as its prey.

Name: Black Belt
Description: You may use this card only if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent. During this turn, each of your Active Pokémon’s attacks does 40 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Indigo Plateau
Description: Each Pokémon LEGEND in play (both yours and your opponent’s) gets +30 HP.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Junk Arm
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. Search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. You can’t choose Junk Arm with the effect of this card.

Name: Seeker
Description: Each player returns 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it to his or her hand. (You return your Pokémon first.)
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Twins
Description: You may use this card only if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent. Search your deck for any 2 cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Rescue Energy
Description: Rescue Energy provides [C] Energy. If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack, put that Pokémon back into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)

Name: Absol
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Absol is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent puts a Basic Pokémon from his or her hand onto his or her Bench, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: Choose 1 Pokémon from your hand and put it in the Lost Zone. (If you can’t put a Pokémon in the Lost Zone, this attack does nothing.)

Name: Celebi
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Celebi is your Active Pokémon, you may attach a [G] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Celebi is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to Celebi by attacks from your opponent’s Stage 1 or Stage 2 Pokémon.

Name: Electrode
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. If you do, Electrode is Knocked Out. Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. Choose as many Energy cards as you like and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Discard the other cards. This power can’t be used if Electrode is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Gengar
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Gengar is your Active Pokémon, if any of your opponent’s Pokémon would be Knocked Out, put that Pokémon in the Lost Zone instead of discarding. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)
Effects: Look at your opponent’s hand and choose a number of Pokémon you find there up to the number of [P] Energy attached to Gengar. Put the Pokémon you chose in the Lost Zone.
Put 4 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Machamp
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Machamp is on your Bench, you may move all [F] Energy attached to your Active Pokémon to Machamp. If you do, switch Machamp with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Discard a Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Does 100 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

Name: Magnezone
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. This power can’t be used if Magnezone is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Put as many Energy cards attached to your Pokémon as you like in the Lost Zone. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Energy cards put in the Lost Zone in this way.

Name: Mew
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Mew can use the attacks of all Pokémon in the Lost Zone (both yours and your opponent’s). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Effects: Search your deck for 1 Pokémon and put it in the Lost Zone. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Yanmega
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, the attack cost of each of Yanmega’s attacks is 0.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.

Name: Darkrai & Cresselia LEGEND
Effects: Choose 2 Energy attached to Darkrai & Cresselia LEGEND and put them in the Lost Zone. If any of your opponent’s Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from this attack, put that Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone instead of discarding it.
Move as many damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon as you like to any of your opponent’s other Pokémon in any way you like.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Darkrai & Cresselia LEGEND.
